originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&buf` is never used.

reasoning: Line 26: `buf = malloc(bufsize)`. When CLEAN_UP=F, the #ifdef block at L29-33 is not compiled, so buf is assigned but never read or freed — dead store (and memory leak). When CLEAN_UP=T, buf is used at L30 and freed at L31, so no dead store. The '// ERROR' at L31 marks the VBDB-known bug (uninitialized branch, CLEAN_UP=T path); this dead store (CLEAN_UP=F) is a distinct new bug not in VBDB. PC = CLEAN_UP=F exactly matches the condition under which the dead store occurs. Classification: TRUE_NEW.
